question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: load(FileID, long)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted consensusTimestamp far in the past to force load() to retrieve an archaic throttleDefinitionFile version with severely restrictive TPS limits, causing network transaction throughput to drop below the threshold that constitutes a 500%+ block delay?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: loadWithRetry(FileID, long, SystemFile)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger repeated ParseException failures across all 9 retries of retryTemplate, exhausting the retry budget and forcing fallback to systemFile.genesisFile() which contains genesis-era throttle definitions with near-zero TPS, effectively freezing network transaction processing for the duration of the block?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: loadWithRetry(FileID, long, SystemFile)] Can an unprivileged external user cause the nanoSeconds.set(fileData.getConsensusTimestamp() - 1) timestamp regression in each retry iteration to walk the timestamp back to a point where only genesis throttle definitions exist, causing the node to apply genesis-era transaction limits and temporarily freeze block production?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: roundDownToHour(long)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a consensusTimestamp just after an hour boundary so that roundDownToHour() returns the previous hour's start, causing the exchangeRateFile or throttleDefinitionFile loaded to be up to 3600 seconds stale, and if that stale version contains restrictive throttle definitions, transactions are frozen for the entire remainder of that hour window?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: load(FileID, long)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that load() skips the database entirely for genesisNetworkProperties (returning the genesis file unconditionally) to ensure that network property overrides that would increase block throughput are never applied, causing block times to remain at genesis-era levels and exceed 500% of the 24-hour average?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: loadAll()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a race condition on the lazy-initialized getSystemFiles() map during high-concurrency block processing, causing loadAll() to be invoked multiple times simultaneously and returning inconsistent throttleDefinitionFile or feeScheduleFile versions that stall transaction validation and delay block finalization beyond 500% of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: load(FileID, long)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a consensusTimestamp of 0 or a negative value to cause roundDownToHour() to produce an epoch-zero cache key, forcing the system to load the oldest possible throttleDefinitionFile from the database (or fall back to genesis), applying genesis throttle limits and freezing transaction processing for the affected block?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: load(FileID, long)] Can an unprivileged external user poison the CacheKey(fileId, consensusTimestamp) cache entry for the throttleDefinitionFile by triggering a load with a specific rounded timestamp, inserting a genesis-era throttle file into the cache, so that all subsequent block-processing requests within that hour window retrieve the restrictive cached version and experience 500%+ block delays?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: loadWithRetry(FileID, long, SystemFile)] Can an unprivileged external user cause the AtomicLong nanoSeconds to be decremented 9 times (once per retry) by injecting malformed file data that always triggers ParseException, walking the effective timestamp back by up to 9 nanoseconds and potentially crossing a throttle-definition update boundary, causing an older restrictive throttle file to be applied and block times to spike?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: loadFromDB(FileID, long)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any rate-limiting or authentication on loadFromDB() to flood the system with requests for the throttleDefinitionFile at varying timestamps, exhausting database connection pool resources and causing block-processing threads to stall, delaying block production by 500%+ of the 24-hour average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: load(FileID, long)] Can an unprivileged external user submit requests with consensusTimestamps that all round down to the same hour boundary, causing a thundering-herd cache miss on the CacheKey for the throttleDefinitionFile, overwhelming the database with simultaneous getFileAtTimestamp() calls and delaying block finalization beyond 500% of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: loadWithRetry(FileID, long, SystemFile)] Can an unprivileged external user cause the retryTemplate to execute all 9 retries for the throttleDefinitionFile by ensuring each getFileAtTimestamp() call returns a file with a ParseException-triggering payload, consuming significant CPU and I/O per block and cumulatively delaying block production to exceed 500% of the 24-hour average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: loadAll()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the lazy initialization of getSystemFiles() at a moment when the throttleDefinitionFile database record is temporarily unavailable (e.g., during a maintenance window), causing loadAll() to store a genesis-era throttle file in the map permanently for the lifetime of the JVM, freezing transaction throughput for all subsequent blocks?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: maxExpiry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the use of Instant.now() in maxExpiry() to cause a time-of-check/time-of-use discrepancy where a file loaded with a far-future expiry is treated as valid even when its throttle definitions are outdated, causing the node to apply stale throttle limits and delay blocks by 500%+ of the 24-hour average?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: load(FileID, long)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a consensusTimestamp that is exactly at an hour boundary to cause roundDownToHour() to return the same value, bypassing the intended one-hour granularity and loading a throttleDefinitionFile from a period when throttle limits were at their most restrictive, causing block delays exceeding 500% of the 24-hour average?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: loadWithRetry(FileID, long, SystemFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the RetryException catch block in loadWithRetry() silently returns systemFile.genesisFile() without any alerting or circuit-breaking, allowing an attacker to persistently trigger retry exhaustion and cause the node to always apply genesis throttle definitions, freezing transactions across multiple consecutive blocks?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: load(FileID, long)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of input validation on the consensusTimestamp parameter in load() to pass Long.MAX_VALUE, causing roundDownToHour() to overflow and produce a negative or zero timestamp, which then retrieves an incorrect throttleDefinitionFile version and applies wrong throttle limits that freeze block production?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: roundDownToHour(long)] Can an unprivileged external user pass a consensusTimestamp of Long.MIN_VALUE to roundDownToHour(), causing integer overflow in the division and multiplication, producing an unexpected cache key that maps to a non-existent or genesis-era throttleDefinitionFile, causing the node to apply genesis throttle limits and freeze block production?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: load(FileID, long)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the dual-cache-manager design (exchangeRatesCacheManager vs defaultSystemFileCacheManager) by crafting requests that cause the throttleDefinitionFile to be cached under the wrong cache manager, resulting in cache misses on every block-processing request and repeated expensive database lookups that delay block finalization beyond 500% of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: loadWithRetry(FileID, long, SystemFile)] Can an unprivileged external user cause the AtomicInteger attempt counter to reach its maximum value during retry exhaustion for the throttleDefinitionFile, and if the retry logic has no backoff delay, cause a tight retry loop that monopolizes the block-processing thread and delays block production by 500%+ of the 24-hour average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: load(FileID, long)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that cache.get(cacheKey, File.class) returns null on a cache miss (rather than blocking) to create a cache stampede where thousands of concurrent block-processing threads simultaneously attempt to load the throttleDefinitionFile from the database, overwhelming the DB and causing block delays exceeding 500% of the 24-hour average?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: loadAll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the order of file loading in loadAll() — where throttleDefinitionFile is loaded last — to cause a partial initialization state where earlier system files (e.g., feeScheduleFile) are loaded but throttleDefinitionFile is not yet available, causing block-processing to proceed with no throttle definitions and either freeze or allow unbounded transaction flooding?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: load(FileID, long)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that load() logs cache updates at INFO level (log.info) but does not log cache misses at a detectable level, allowing an attacker to silently cause repeated cache misses for the throttleDefinitionFile without triggering any alerting, while the repeated DB lookups cumulatively delay block production beyond 500% of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: loadWithRetry(FileID, long, SystemFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that loadWithRetry() catches only InvalidFileException (wrapping ParseException) and not other runtime exceptions, causing an unexpected exception during throttleDefinitionFile parsing to propagate uncaught and crash the block-processing thread, halting block production entirely and exceeding 500% of the 24-hour average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: load(FileID, long)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of synchronization on the cache.put(cacheKey, result) call in load() to cause a race condition where two threads simultaneously load different versions of the throttleDefinitionFile and the older, more restrictive version overwrites the newer one in the cache, causing all subsequent block-processing requests to use the restrictive throttle definitions and delay blocks by 500%+?
